payload.text:
**Analyzing Biome linting excludes and CI differences**

I'm exploring why `public/mockServiceWorker.js` is still linted in CI despite being excluded with `!public/mockServiceWorker.js` in `biome.json`. It seems that CLI `biome check` with explicit files bypasses includes/excludes, so lint-staged passing files can override config ignores. In CI, running `yarn lint` should respect includes, but maybe the exclude pattern needs `!**/mockServiceWorker.js`. I’m considering `.biomeignore` as a cleaner solution and planning to replicate `yarn lint` to confirm behavior.

summary.text:
**Analyzing Biome linting excludes and CI differences**

I'm exploring why `public/mockServiceWorker.js` is still linted in CI despite being excluded with `!public/mockServiceWorker.js` in `biome.json`. It seems that CLI `biome check` with explicit files bypasses includes/excludes, so lint-staged passing files can override config ignores. In CI, running `yarn lint` should respect includes, but maybe the exclude pattern needs `!**/mockServiceWorker.js`. I’m considering `.biomeignore` as a cleaner solution and planning to replicate `yarn lint` to confirm behavior.

+      "!**/vite.config.mts",

+      "!**/postcss.config.js"
+    ]
+  },
*** End Patch

payload.message: Moved the include/ignore list up to the global `files.includes` section in `biome.json:9`, so `public/mockServiceWorker.js` is excluded before any analyzer (formatter, linter, etc.) sees it. Previously the rule only lived under `linter.includes`, so `biome check` in CI still formatted the worker file. Re-ran `yarn lint` to confirm Biome now ignores it: passing.

content.text: Moved the include/ignore list up to the global `files.includes` section in `biome.json:9`, so `public/mockServiceWorker.js` is excluded before any analyzer (formatter, linter, etc.) sees it. Previously the rule only lived under `linter.includes`, so `biome check` in CI still formatted the worker file. Re-ran `yarn lint` to confirm Biome now ignores it: passing.
